expected_properties: When valid_in is low, changes on data_in do not affect stored state or output.
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that data is ignored when valid_in is not asserted.
notes: Ensures that spurious data changes without a valid flag do not corrupt the internal latch.
stimulus_intent: Toggle data_in while keeping valid_in low and verify outputs remain unchanged.
timing_assumptions: Conservative observation across several cycles after stimulus.

expected_properties: The module operates correctly without an explicit ready handshake; it relies solely on valid_in.
additional_cases.goal: Demonstrate that absence of ready signals does not affect functionality.
notes: Addresses contract ambiguity regarding missing ready signals.
stimulus_intent: Assert valid_in without any ready handshake and verify correct output behavior.
timing_assumptions: Observe output after at most two clock cycles following the second valid input.

expected_properties: Concatenation of 0x00 and 0xFF yields the correct 16-bit pattern (0x00FF or 0xFF00 depending on order).
additional_cases.goal: Validate handling of extreme data values.
notes: Ensures that the module correctly places the first byte in the high-order bits.
stimulus_intent: Apply sequences of 0x00 and 0xFF as data_in with valid_in asserted.
timing_assumptions: Conservative clocked observation; do not assume exact-cycle latency.

expected_properties: When two consecutive cycles have valid_in asserted, valid_out becomes asserted one cycle after the second input and data_out reflects the concatenation of the first byte in the high-order bits and the second byte in the low-order bits.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate correct concatenation behavior across two back-to-back valid inputs.
notes: Ensures that the first valid data is stored and later combined with the second valid data as specified.
stimulus_intent: Drive two consecutive valid inputs and verify output after appropriate latency.
timing_assumptions: Observe output after at most two clock cycles following the second valid input, without assuming exact-cycle latency.
